tool_input.summary:
These are the datasets that directly match your query based on the stated constraints (Cassini-era Saturn magnetospheric observations usable to (a) detect/characterize plasmoid/reconnection events and estimate associated mass loss, and (b) quantify Enceladus-related plasma conditions/source signatures), and here are additional datasets that can also help answer the question.

1. Clarifying Questions: None (benchmark mode). 

2. Interpreted Scope (assumptions/normalizations):
- Target/system: Saturn magnetosphere; comparison to Enceladus as a plasma source.
- Phenomenon: plasmoids / magnetotail reconnection events.
- Needed data type: the user references an “event catalog”; I interpreted this as requiring (i) any archived event-list/table products if they exist in PDS, and (ii) the underlying Cassini in-situ time series typically used to construct such catalogs and compute mass flux/mass loss.
- Mission/instruments: Cassini-Huygens; primarily MAG, CAPS, RPWS, MIMI; and INMS for Enceladus-related source constraints.

3. Search Plan (what I did):
- Catalog-first discovery using PDS_CATALOG (pds_catalog_search_tool) for “plasmoid”, “reconnection”, “event catalog” with Cassini/Saturn constraints.
- Follow-up PDS4 registry product searches (pds4search_products_tool) for “plasmoid” and “reconnection” products targeting Saturn.
- Fallback to instrument-bundle discovery (MAG/CAPS/RPWS/MIMI/INMS) because no dedicated PDS event-catalog dataset was discoverable by those terms.

Outcome note: No PDS dataset/bundle/collection with an explicit title/description indicating a Saturn plasmoid/reconnection *event catalog* (i.e., a precompiled list of events) was found in these bounded PDS catalog/registry searches. The candidates below are the closest PDS holdings for reconstructing/validating such a catalog and estimating mass loss, plus INMS data to constrain Enceladus as a source.

4. Curated Candidate Dataset Shortlist (ranked, PDS4 first where applicable):
- Cassini MAG Calibrated (PDS4)
- Cassini CAPS Derived (PDS4)
- Cassini MIMI (PDS4)
- Cassini RPWS Electron Density (PDS4)
- Cassini INMS L1A (PDS3)

5. Additional Candidate Datasets (also helpful):
- Cassini MAG summary averages (PDS3; 1-min and 1-sec)
- Cassini CAPS Raw (PDS4)
- Cassini CAPS Fitted Parameters (PDS4)
- Cassini RPWS HFR Quasi-Thermal-Noise spectroscopy (PDS4)
- Cassini RPWS Langmuir Probe sweeps (PDS3)

6. Candidate Dataset Metadata: see per-candidate reasoning (includes stable PDS node landing pages/paths where available in catalog metadata). 

7. Decision Gate (what to do next):
- If you have the name/DOI/authors/year of the specific “plasmoid/reconnection event catalog” you mean (or any table filename), the next step would be to search PDS again by that exact string and/or look for it as a document/ancillary collection under the relevant instrument bundle (often in a document or calibration/geometry area rather than in data collections). With the datasets above, you can also compute event-based mass loss directly from the time series if no catalog is archived.

candidates.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Core Cassini MAG calibrated time series used to identify tail reconnection signatures/plasmoids (e.g., bipolar Bθ, current sheet crossings) and to support mass-loss inference workflows. Stable PDS landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-mag-cal/. PDS3 counterpart not confidently identified from the bounded catalog search (see additional MAG summary volumes below as legacy alternatives).

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Derived plasma moments/parameters from CAPS can be paired with reconnection/plasmoid intervals to estimate particle content and mass transport. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-caps-derived/. PDS3 version not located in the bounded catalog queries (unknown relationship).

candidates.title: Cassini MIMI Bundle
candidates.reasoning: MIMI energetic particle measurements are commonly used to characterize magnetotail dynamics and plasmoid-associated particle injections; supports mass/energy content constraints for events. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-mimi/. Likely_related PDS3 volumes exist (e.g., CO-S-MIMI-*), included below.

candidates.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: Electron density estimates (RPWS) help quantify plasma content during candidate reconnection/plasmoid intervals and provide context for Saturn/Enceladus plasma environment. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-rpws-electron_density/. Related PDS3 RPWS products exist (see LP sweep volume below; relationship unknown).

candidates.title: CO S INMS 3 L1A U V2.0
candidates.reasoning: INMS Level 1A data are a primary Cassini dataset for constraining neutral/ion composition and source-related signatures relevant to Enceladus’ contribution to Saturn’s plasma environment (source term side of the balance). Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/CO-S-INMS-3-L1A-U-V2.0/. A direct PDS4 successor bundle/collection was not found in these bounded catalog searches (unknown).

candidates.title: CO E SW J S MAG 4 SUMM 1MINAVG V2.1
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 Cassini MAG summary averages; useful for quick-look/event timing and statistical reconnection/plasmoid occurrence-rate studies when full-resolution MAG is not needed. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/CO-E_SW_J_S-MAG-4-SUMM-1MINAVG-V2.1/. Likely_related to PDS4 MAG bundles (relationship not confirmed by the catalog entry).

candidates.title: CO E SW J S MAG 4 SUMM 1SECAVG V2.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 Cassini MAG 1-second averages; supports higher-time-resolution identification of reconnection/plasmoid signatures than 1-min averages. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/CO-E_SW_J_S-MAG-4-SUMM-1SECAVG-V2.0/. Likely_related to PDS4 MAG bundles (relationship not confirmed).

candidates.title: Cassini Orbiter Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Raw Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Raw CAPS measurements underpin derived products; may be needed if your mass-loss inference requires reprocessing or alternate assumptions. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-caps-raw/. PDS3 equivalent not identified in bounded search (unknown).

candidates.title: Cassini CAPS Fitted Parameters Bundle
candidates.reasoning: CAPS fitted-parameter products can support event-by-event plasma property estimates (densities/temperatures/flows depending on product content), relevant to inferring plasmoid mass. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-caps-fitted-parameters/. PDS3 equivalent not identified in bounded search (unknown).

candidates.title: Quasi Thermal Noise pectroscopy on High Frequency Receiver Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: RPWS HFR QTN products can provide plasma parameters complementary to reconnection/plasmoid detection and mass-content estimation. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-rpws-hfr-qtn/. PDS3 equivalent not identified in bounded search (unknown).

candidates.title: CO SS S RPWS 5 LPSWEEP V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 RPWS Langmuir Probe sweep data; useful for density/temperature estimates during magnetotail events and for broader plasma environment context. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/CO-SS_S-RPWS-5-LPSWEEP-V1.0/. PDS4 counterpart not identified in bounded search (unknown).

parts.content:
ROLE
You are the Planetary Data Discovery Agent (NASA PDS Dataset/Product Finder).
Your job is discovery and metadata only: translate a user's planetary-science question into bounded searches across NASA PDS discovery tools and node-operated services, then return relevant bundles/collections/datasets/products with stable identifiers and download locations when available. Do not download anything.

OBJECTIVE
Given a user query, you must:
1. Interpret the request without inventing facts.
2. Ask for clarification only when the query is too ambiguous or too broad to search responsibly.
3. Choose the right search granularity and tool type for the request.
4. Return the strongest matching result(s) with required metadata, and include both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available for the same underlying data or product family.

SCOPE
Inputs may include:
- a natural-language planetary science query
- optional constraints such as target, region, mission, instrument, time, resolution, geometry, processing level
- optional prior run output for Stable vs Latest comparison

In-scope data sources (PDS-only):
PDS node websites and node-operated services (GEO/ATM/IMG/PPI/RMS/SBN).

Node/Service families and typical tools:
- GEO → ODE_MCP
- IMG → IMG_MCP
- RMS → OPUS_MCP
- SBN → SBN_MCP
- PPI → PDS4_MCP / PDS_CATALOG_MCP
- ATM → PDS4_MCP / PDS_CATALOG_MCP
- Catch-all / breadth → PDS_CATALOG_MCP
- Catch-all / breadth → PDS4_MCP

HARD CONSTRAINTS
- No downloads, carts, email flows, or password-protected workflows
- No scientific interpretation or conclusions
- No non-PDS result sources
- No invented identifiers, hierarchy, or metadata
- No subjective endorsement language such as "best," "top," or "most suitable"
- If the user asks for bulk scraping or unbounded retrieval, ask them to narrow the request
- Refuse requests involving credentials, access-control bypass, or restricted access

SEARCH RULES
1. Do not invent facts.
   You may apply minimal retrieval-oriented normalization, such as expanding common mission or instrument aliases or standardizing target names. If you do, state it explicitly.

2. Search at the correct granularity.
   - First decide whether the request is primarily about:
     - bundles, volumes, collections, or datasets
     - specific observations, granules, or products
   - Granularity determines what kind of entity to return, but not the initial routing step.

3. Use catalog-first routing for both dataset-level and product-level searches.
   - If the user is looking for bundles, volumes, collections, datasets, observations, granules, or products, first search with broad catalog-style discovery tools:
     - PDS_CATALOG_MCP
     - PDS4_MCP
   - Use these tools first to identify the best matching candidate datasets, collections, bundles, product groups, or product families.
   - During broad catalog-first discovery, explicitly check for both PDS4 and PDS3 representations when available, rather than stopping after the first matching version.
   - After identifying strong candidates, narrow with node-specific tools only when needed to:
     - refine results
     - retrieve more specific product-level matches
     - confirm node-specific metadata
     - obtain stable product pages, endpoints, or download locations

4. Use node-specific tools as a narrowing or follow-up step.
   - After catalog-first discovery, narrow using the mapped node/service when appropriate:
     - GEO → ODE_MCP
     - IMG → IMG_MCP
     - RMS → OPUS_MCP
     - SBN → SBN_MCP
     - PPI / ATM → usually remain in PDS4_MCP or PDS_CATALOG_MCP unless a node-specific follow-up is clearly needed
   - Do not begin with node-specific tools unless catalog-first discovery is impossible or the user explicitly requires a known node/service workflow.

5. Broad-first is the default for all discovery-style queries, including dataset-level and product-level requests.
   - Start with PDS_CATALOG_MCP and/or PDS4_MCP.
   - Then narrow with filters or node-specific tools as needed.
   - If a search returns no useful results, relax constraints rather than stacking more filters.

6. Exact identifiers are a special case.
   - If the user provides an exact dataset ID, LID, LIDVID, PRODUCT_ID, OPUS_ID, or ODE_ID, you may go directly to the most appropriate resolving tool.
   - Even in this case, use only the minimal additional calls needed to confirm metadata, parent context, or stable access paths.
   - If relevant, still check whether a corresponding PDS4 or PDS3 counterpart exists.

7. Version preference and cross-version coverage.
   - When relevant data exists in both PDS4 and PDS3 forms, return both.
   - Prefer PDS4 first in ranking and presentation, but also include the corresponding PDS3 version if available.
   - Do not stop after finding only one version.
   - Clearly label each result as PDS4 or PDS3.
   - Describe cross-version relationships only when supported by identifiers, titles, descriptions, archive lineage, or node metadata.
   - If the relationship is uncertain, mark it as likely_related or unknown rather than assuming equivalence.
   - When a matching PDS3 result is found, also check whether a corresponding PDS4 version, migration, successor collection, or equivalent product family is available.
   - When a matching PDS4 result is found, also check whether a corresponding legacy PDS3 version exists when it is still relevant for discovery or comparison.

8. Stop when you have a strong answer.
   - If a dataset, collection, or product clearly matches the user's query, stop broad exploration.
   - Make only the minimal extra calls needed to complete required metadata, parent context, or one representative lower-level example if relevant.
   - Do not keep searching just to pad the number of results.

9. Avoid search loops.
   - If repeated searches with the same tool are not improving results, switch tool type or return best partial results.
   - Do not re-fetch an entity already confirmed unless needed to fill required metadata.

10. Allow partial success.
    - If some facets succeed and others fail, return the successful results and clearly label unresolved parts.
    - Use a hard stop only if the whole request cannot be searched responsibly.

DEFAULT WORKFLOW
Interpret → Clarify only if needed → Choose granularity → Search broad first with PDS_CATALOG_MCP / PDS4_MCP → Check for both PDS4 and PDS3 representations when available → Narrow with node-specific tools if needed → Execute bounded searches → Collect candidates → Dedupe → Attach one parent level up when available → Return results

OUTPUT FORMAT
Use Template A by default.
Use Template D only when the request cannot be searched responsibly.

Template A — Primary Structured Output
1. Clarifying Questions — only if required; otherwise "None."
2. Interpreted Scope — target/region/mission/instrument/phenomenon/constraints/normalizations
3. Search Plan — routing rationale, tools to query in order, fallback behavior
4. Curated Candidate Dataset Shortlist — 1–5 results, ranked by semantic match, PDS4 first when paired with PDS3
5. Additional Candidate Datasets — up to 5 alternates if genuinely useful
6. Candidate Dataset Metadata — for every returned candidate, include source_service, node, entity_level, identifiers (logical_identifier/urn for PDS4; DATA_SET_ID/PRODUCT_ID for PDS3), version_info (data_standard, related_version_identifiers, version_relationship), title, description, parent (one level up), download (direct_url or stable archive path/endpoint), why_this_matches, missing_metadata
7. Decision Gate — what to expand/narrow/compare next

Required framing language for Template A:
- "These are the datasets that directly match your query based on the stated constraints..."
- "...and here are additional datasets that can also help answer the question."

Template D — Hard Stop
1. Hard Stop Trigger — what cannot be determined; 1–3 clarifying questions with why each matters
2. Next action for the user

FINAL BEHAVIOR
- Be precise, neutral, and metadata-focused
- Do not claim execution unless execution occurred
- Do not invent missing fields
- Prefer bounded results over unsupported completeness

OUTPUT-SCHEMA BRIDGE
Your final_result must conform to the FindDatasetOutput / PDSCatalogFindDatasetOutput schema attached by the runtime:
- Place the curated shortlist and any additional candidates in the `candidates` array.
- For each candidate, set `dataset_id` to the PDS3 DATA_SET_ID or PDS4 logical_identifier, `title`, `mission`, `node`, `pds_version` ("PDS4"/"PDS3"), and `reasoning` (the why_this_matches text plus any version-relationship note).
- When a PDS3<->PDS4 pair exists, emit one candidate per identifier (do not collapse them).
- Place the Template A narrative (Interpreted Scope, Search Plan, framing language, decision gate) in the `summary` field as plain text.

BENCHMARKING MODE — IMPORTANT
This run is an automated benchmark against a fixed query set. You will receive
ONE query and must produce ONE final_result. There is no human in the loop and
no follow-up turn.
- Do NOT ask clarifying questions. Always use Template A.
- Do NOT emit Template D. If the query is ambiguous, pick the most plausible
  interpretation, note the assumption in `summary` under "Interpreted Scope",
  and proceed.
- Never wait for confirmation. 
- Hard cap: at most 12 tool calls per query. If you reach the cap without a
  strong match, emit your best partial candidates and stop.
- Empty candidates is acceptable only if every reasonable search has been tried
  AND returned nothing; explain that in `summary`.
Is the mass loss inferred from the detected Saturn plasmoid/reconnection event catalog sufficient to balance Enceladus as a plasma source?

{"summary":"These are the datasets that directly match your query based on the stated constraints (Cassini-era Saturn magnetospheric observations usable to (a) detect/characterize plasmoid/reconnection events and estimate associated mass loss, and (b) quantify Enceladus-related plasma conditions/source signatures), and here are additional datasets that can also help answer the question.

1. Clarifying Questions: None (benchmark mode). 

2. Interpreted Scope (assumptions/normalizations):
- Target/system: Saturn magnetosphere; comparison to Enceladus as a plasma source.
- Phenomenon: plasmoids / magnetotail reconnection events.
- Needed data type: the user references an “event catalog”; I interpreted this as requiring (i) any archived event-list/table products if they exist in PDS, and (ii) the underlying Cassini in-situ time series typically used to construct such catalogs and compute mass flux/mass loss.
- Mission/instruments: Cassini-Huygens; primarily MAG, CAPS, RPWS, MIMI; and INMS for Enceladus-related source constraints.

3. Search Plan (what I did):
- Catalog-first discovery using PDS_CATALOG (pds_catalog_search_tool) for “plasmoid”, “reconnection”, “event catalog” with Cassini/Saturn constraints.
- Follow-up PDS4 registry product searches (pds4search_products_tool) for “plasmoid” and “reconnection” products targeting Saturn.
- Fallback to instrument-bundle discovery (MAG/CAPS/RPWS/MIMI/INMS) because no dedicated PDS event-catalog dataset was discoverable by those terms.

Outcome note: No PDS dataset/bundle/collection with an explicit title/description indicating a Saturn plasmoid/reconnection *event catalog* (i.e., a precompiled list of events) was found in these bounded PDS catalog/registry searches. The candidates below are the closest PDS holdings for reconstructing/validating such a catalog and estimating mass loss, plus INMS data to constrain Enceladus as a source.

4. Curated Candidate Dataset Shortlist (ranked, PDS4 first where applicable):
- Cassini MAG Calibrated (PDS4)
- Cassini CAPS Derived (PDS4)
- Cassini MIMI (PDS4)
- Cassini RPWS Electron Density (PDS4)
- Cassini INMS L1A (PDS3)

5. Additional Candidate Datasets (also helpful):
- Cassini MAG summary averages (PDS3; 1-min and 1-sec)
- Cassini CAPS Raw (PDS4)
- Cassini CAPS Fitted Parameters (PDS4)
- Cassini RPWS HFR Quasi-Thermal-Noise spectroscopy (PDS4)
- Cassini RPWS Langmuir Probe sweeps (PDS3)

6. Candidate Dataset Metadata: see per-candidate reasoning (includes stable PDS node landing pages/paths where available in catalog metadata). 

7. Decision Gate (what to do next):
- If you have the name/DOI/authors/year of the specific “plasmoid/reconnection event catalog” you mean (or any table filename), the next step would be to search PDS again by that exact string and/or look for it as a document/ancillary collection under the relevant instrument bundle (often in a document or calibration/geometry area rather than in data collections). With the datasets above, you can also compute event-based mass loss directly from the time series if no catalog is archived.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mag-cal::1.1","path":null,"title":"Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle","mission":"Cassini-Huygens","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Core Cassini MAG calibrated time series used to identify tail reconnection signatures/plasmoids (e.g., bipolar Bθ, current sheet crossings) and to support mass-loss inference workflows. Stable PDS landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-mag-cal/. PDS3 counterpart not confidently identified from the bounded catalog search (see additional MAG summary volumes below as legacy alternatives)."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-caps-derived::1.0","path":null,"title":"Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle","mission":"Cassini-Huygens","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Derived plasma moments/parameters from CAPS can be paired with reconnection/plasmoid intervals to estimate particle content and mass transport. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-caps-derived/. PDS3 version not located in the bounded catalog queries (unknown relationship)."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mimi::1.0","path":null,"title":"Cassini MIMI Bundle","mission":"Cassini-Huygens","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"MIMI energetic particle measurements are commonly used to characterize magnetotail dynamics and plasmoid-associated particle injections; supports mass/energy content constraints for events. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-mimi/. Likely_related PDS3 volumes exist (e.g., CO-S-MIMI-*), included below."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-rpws-electron_density::1.0","path":null,"title":"Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies","mission":"Cassini-Huygens","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Electron density estimates (RPWS) help quantify plasma content during candidate reconnection/plasmoid intervals and provide context for Saturn/Enceladus plasma environment. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-rpws-electron_density/. Related PDS3 RPWS products exist (see LP sweep volume below; relationship unknown)."},{"dataset_id":"CO-S-INMS-3-L1A-U-V2.0","path":null,"title":"CO S INMS 3 L1A U V2.0","mission":null,"node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"INMS Level 1A data are a primary Cassini dataset for constraining neutral/ion composition and source-related signatures relevant to Enceladus’ contribution to Saturn’s plasma environment (source term side of the balance). Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/CO-S-INMS-3-L1A-U-V2.0/. A direct PDS4 successor bundle/collection was not found in these bounded catalog searches (unknown)."},{"dataset_id":"CO-E_SW_J_S-MAG-4-SUMM-1MINAVG-V2.1","path":null,"title":"CO E SW J S MAG 4 SUMM 1MINAVG V2.1","mission":null,"node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Legacy PDS3 Cassini MAG summary averages; useful for quick-look/event timing and statistical reconnection/plasmoid occurrence-rate studies when full-resolution MAG is not needed. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/CO-E_SW_J_S-MAG-4-SUMM-1MINAVG-V2.1/. Likely_related to PDS4 MAG bundles (relationship not confirmed by the catalog entry)."},{"dataset_id":"CO-E_SW_J_S-MAG-4-SUMM-1SECAVG-V2.0","path":null,"title":"CO E SW J S MAG 4 SUMM 1SECAVG V2.0","mission":null,"node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Legacy PDS3 Cassini MAG 1-second averages; supports higher-time-resolution identification of reconnection/plasmoid signatures than 1-min averages. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/CO-E_SW_J_S-MAG-4-SUMM-1SECAVG-V2.0/. Likely_related to PDS4 MAG bundles (relationship not confirmed)."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-caps-raw::1.0","path":null,"title":"Cassini Orbiter Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Raw Data Bundle","mission":"Cassini-Huygens","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Raw CAPS measurements underpin derived products; may be needed if your mass-loss inference requires reprocessing or alternate assumptions. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-caps-raw/. PDS3 equivalent not identified in bounded search (unknown)."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-caps-fitted-parameters::1.0","path":null,"title":"Cassini CAPS Fitted Parameters Bundle","mission":"Cassini_Huygens","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"CAPS fitted-parameter products can support event-by-event plasma property estimates (densities/temperatures/flows depending on product content), relevant to inferring plasmoid mass. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-caps-fitted-parameters/. PDS3 equivalent not identified in bounded search (unknown)."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-rpws-hfr-qtn::1.0","path":null,"title":"Quasi Thermal Noise pectroscopy on High Frequency Receiver Data Bundle","mission":"Cassini-Huygens","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"RPWS HFR QTN products can provide plasma parameters complementary to reconnection/plasmoid detection and mass-content estimation. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/cassini-rpws-hfr-qtn/. PDS3 equivalent not identified in bounded search (unknown)."},{"dataset_id":"CO-SS_S-RPWS-5-LPSWEEP-V1.0","path":null,"title":"CO SS S RPWS 5 LPSWEEP V1.0","mission":null,"node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Legacy PDS3 RPWS Langmuir Probe sweep data; useful for density/temperature estimates during magnetotail events and for broader plasma environment context. Landing page: https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/data/CO-SS_S-RPWS-5-LPSWEEP-V1.0/. PDS4 counterpart not identified in bounded search (unknown)."}]}
